title: Real or Generated: You Decide

modules.title: The Image That Broke the Internet
modules.sub: Uncover how one viral photo sparked school-wide chaos
modules.description: Learners are dropped into a mystery: a shocking image is spreading around school and no one can agree if it is real. They examine the image and initial reactions, forming their first hypothesis about whether it was AI-generated or authentic.

modules.title: Clues Hidden in AI-Made Pictures
modules.sub: Spot the weird details AI images always seem to leave behind
modules.description: Learners study a set of suspect images and hunt for telltale AI artifacts — distorted hands, strange backgrounds, unnatural lighting, and symmetry glitches. By the end, they build a personal checklist of visual red flags to carry into future investigations.

modules.title: When the Words Sound Too Perfect
modules.sub: Detect the patterns that give AI-written text away
modules.description: Learners read and compare articles and social-media posts, some written by humans and some generated by AI, looking for clues like repetitive phrasing, oddly flawless grammar, and a lack of genuine personal voice. They practise flagging suspicious passages and explain their reasoning to a classmate character in the story.

modules.title: Deepfakes, Voices, and Video Tricks
modules.sub: Learn how AI can fake faces, voices, and moving footage
modules.description: Learners watch short video clips and audio samples, learning to spot deepfake warning signs such as unnatural blinking, mismatched lip-sync, and audio artefacts. They decide which clips from the school mystery could have been manipulated and justify their verdicts.

modules.title: The Verification Toolkit in Action
modules.sub: Use real fact-checking strategies to test any media claim
modules.description: Learners are introduced to practical verification tools and methods — reverse image search, metadata inspection, cross-referencing sources, and lateral reading. They apply the full toolkit to the original mystery image and other evidence, piecing together what actually happened.

modules.title: Create a Fake â Then Expose It
modules.sub: Make AI content yourself, then write the guide to catch it
modules.description: Learners use an approved AI tool to generate an image, a short text, or a voice clip of their own design. They then write a detection guide explaining exactly what artefacts or clues would reveal their creation as AI-generated, turning their maker experience into advice that helps others stay media-savvy.

synopsis: A viral image has caused chaos at your school â but was it real? Work through a mystery where you examine photos, articles, and video clips, learning to spot the telltale signs of AI generation before the truth gets buried. The final challenge asks you to create a piece of AI content and then write the guide that helps others detect it.
rationale: With AI image, text, and video generation now freely accessible, students encounter synthetic media daily but lack the perceptual and analytical skills to distinguish it from authentic content.
learning_outcome: Students can apply detection strategies to identify likely AI-generated images, text, and video by recognizing characteristic artifacts, and can articulate why synthetic media requires skeptical evaluation.
source_signals: Modern-Day Oracles or Bullshit Machines? How to thrive in a ChatGPT world
Digital Citizenship: Teaching Teens to Navigate the online World Responsibly | BWWT Ep. 240
Tali Horowitz: The Key Issue with AI Chatbots and Teens
Tali Horowitz: How Teens Are Really Using AI
